newSkills.name: Skill Vetter
newSkills.description: Scans skills for malicious code patterns before installation. 3.5K+ downloads. Growing relevance given the security landscape — ClawHub recently purged 2,400+ suspicious skills after the ClawHavoc campaign.
newSkills.notes: Highly recommended for our setup. With 37.8% skills flagged suspicious by ClawScan and Trail of Bits (June 3) demonstrating scanner bypasses, an extra layer of vetting is prudent. Open source, no external API dependency.

newSkills.name: Firecrawl CLI
newSkills.description: Advanced web extraction: scrape, crawl, search, and run browser sessions on any website. Supplements our existing web_fetch with headless browser capability.
newSkills.notes: Powerful but requires Firecrawl API key. Evaluate whether existing web_fetch + agent-browser skill already covers this use case. Medium priority.

newSkills.name: Mcporter
newSkills.description: Install and manage MCP servers from inside OpenClaw. 11.1K downloads. Allows dynamic installation of Model Context Protocol servers.
newSkills.notes: Useful if expanding MCP ecosystem. Dynamic server installation from agent has security implications — evaluate need before installing.

newSkills.name: ChartGen AI
newSkills.description: Recently published data visualization skill. Generates charts and graphs from data. Published by steven-cen on LinkedIn.
newSkills.notes: New skill, low download count, no long-term track record. Our existing diagram-maker skill already covers visualization needs. Skip for now.

newSkills.name: API Gateway
newSkills.description: Managed OAuth to 100+ external APIs (Stripe, Salesforce, HubSpot, etc). 13K downloads.
newSkills.notes: Useful for external service integration, but requires OAuth setup and third-party API keys. Not immediately needed for current workflow.

securitySummary: Security remains the dominant theme. ClawHub now has 52.7K skills, 12M downloads. NVIDIA SkillSpector scans 98% of skills (48.7% advisory rate). Trail of Bits (2026-06-03) published bypasses for ClawHub/Cisco/skills.sh scanners. OpenClaw released 67K-skill security signals dataset on HuggingFace (2026-05-31). ClawScan verdicts: 61.9% clean, 37.8% suspicious, 0.3% malicious. Ongoing crypto-swarm campaign (30 skills from single author). Vetting before installation is critical.
